controls.title: Broken Access Control
controls.description: Access control enforces policy so users cannot act outside their intended permissions. Failures lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction of data.

controls.title: Cryptographic Failures
controls.description: Failures related to cryptography which often lead to sensitive data exposure. Includes use of weak protocols, cleartext transmission, weak keys, or improper certificate validation.

controls.title: Injection
controls.description: Injection flaws such as SQL, NoSQL, OS, and LDAP injection occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query.

controls.title: Insecure Design
controls.description: Risks related to design and architectural flaws. Missing or ineffective control design that cannot be fixed by a perfect implementation.

controls.title: Security Misconfiguration
controls.description: Missing appropriate security hardening across application stack, insecure default configurations, unnecessary features enabled, unpatched flaws.

controls.title: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
controls.description: Components such as libraries, frameworks, and other software modules run with the same privileges as the application. Using vulnerable versions exposes the system.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication Failures
controls.description: Authentication-related attacks such as brute force, credential stuffing, session fixation, and weak password policies.

controls.title: Software and Data Integrity Failures
controls.description: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations, including insecure deserialization and unsigned updates.

controls.title: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
controls.description: Insufficient logging, detection, monitoring, and active response allows attackers to pivot, maintain persistence, and exfiltrate data undetected.

controls.title: Server-Side Request Forgery
controls.description: SSRF flaws occur when a web application fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL, bypassing firewalls and VPNs.
